body:
# 90-Day Plan: Senior Software Engineer — Developer Tooling, Stripe

## Days 1–30: Learn

- **Map the payments request path end-to-end.** Shadow payments infra engineers and trace a live charge from API ingress through routing, authorization, and settlement, documenting each service boundary and ownership team.
- **Audit the existing observability surface.** Inventory the metrics, traces, and logs emitted across the request path — identify gaps where on-call engineers currently rely on manual queries or tribal knowledge rather than instrumented signals.
- **Understand the on-call runbooks.** Read the last 90 days of incident retrospectives and PagerDuty history for the payments infra teams; categorize alert sources by type and frequency to build a data-backed picture of toil.
- **Learn Stripe's internal tooling stack.** Identify the internal dashboards, deployment systems, and developer portals in use by payments teams — understand where the current developer-tooling surface begins and ends and what pain points engineers raise most often.
- **Establish relationships with key partners.** Schedule 1:1s with payments infra team leads and on-call rotators to understand their highest-friction workflows firsthand.

## Days 31–60: Earn

- **Instrument at least one under-observed service in the request path.** Add structured logging and trace spans to a service identified in the observability audit; validate that on-call engineers can answer their most common diagnostic questions from the new signals alone.
- **Ship one on-call toil reduction.** Using the incident retrospective analysis, identify the highest-frequency manual step in an alert response and automate or eliminate it — targeting a measurable reduction in mean time to diagnose (MTTD).
- **Prototype a developer-tooling improvement.** Based on partner feedback, build and demo a scoped improvement to the internal tooling surface — for example, a local payments request replay harness or an enriched trace viewer for charge failures — and collect structured feedback from payments engineers.
- **Establish a baseline metrics dashboard.** Define and publish a shared dashboard tracking on-call load (alert volume, MTTD, escalation rate) and observability coverage across the request path, giving the team a shared language for measuring progress.

## Days 61–90: Compound

- **Land the tooling prototype in production.** Iterate on the prototype from Days 31–60 based on feedback, complete code review with infra partners, and ship to internal users — measure adoption and collect before/after signal on the pain point it targets.
- **Drive a systemic observability gap closure.** Propose and begin executing a prioritized plan to close the top three instrumentation gaps identified in the audit, coordinating with payments infra teams to ensure signal is actionable for on-call rotators.
- **Reduce on-call load by a quantifiable margin.** Target a measurable decrease in alert volume or escalation rate relative to the baseline dashboard — document the interventions and share findings in a team postmortem or internal eng blog post.
- **Define the forward roadmap for developer tooling.** Synthesize learnings from the first 60 days into a written roadmap proposal covering the next two quarters, with prioritized bets on observability, local development experience, and on-call automation — aligned to payments infra team goals.
